body:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="no" ?>
<SnowReport>
	<_LastUpdated>2026-07-15T07:05:25</_LastUpdated>
	<TwentyFourHourSnowfall>0</TwentyFourHourSnowfall>
	<CurrentSnowdepth>44</CurrentSnowdepth>
	<AvSnowdepthinSnowmakingAreas>107</AvSnowdepthinSnowmakingAreas>
	<AvSnowdepthLastMeasuredOn>14/07/2026</AvSnowdepthLastMeasuredOn>

	<SeasonToDateSnowfall>95</SeasonToDateSnowfall>
	<Snowmaking>In Progress</Snowmaking>
	<WeatherConditionsasat6AM></WeatherConditionsasat6AM>
	<Temperature>0</Temperature>
	<OvernightMin>-3</OvernightMin>
	<ForecastMax>6</ForecastMax>
	<Wind>Moderate/Strong 41-60 km/h</Wind>
	<WindDirection>S</WindDirection>
	<Precipitation>None</Precipitation>
	<ExpectedVisibility>Excellent</ExpectedVisibility>
	<ExpectedCloudCover>Mostly Sunny</ExpectedCloudCover>
	<Forecast>Sunny. Winds southerly 35 to 50 km/h tending southwesterly 25 to 35 km/h in the morning then tending southeast to southwesterly 20 to 30 km/h in the early afternoon.</Forecast>
	<ResortReportHeadline>Sun, Guns and Night Skiing Tonight!</ResortReportHeadline>
	<ResortReport><p>After the final snow flurries yesterday afternoon the storm has cleared out, the sky is filled with stars and a there's a stunning sunrise on the way.</p><p>At 6am the temp is bang on zero after an overnight low of -3 and it should max out at a balmy 6 degrees. Right now the wind is a strong southerly bringing the windchill down, making it feel much colder in exposed areas. However this is expected to ease as the day progresses.</p><p>With the clearing skies and drop in temps the snowmakers were able to fire up and they're still going now adding some texture to the cover. The surface feels like it's been turned over well, however just properly assess it as it could be firm in places also.</p><p>It was awesome to get Road Runner up and running yesterday and the same lifts are ready to roll today. If you can't get enough during the day head down to the Big D tonight where the good times will roll on under lights until 8:30pm.</p><p>Have a fun one. Cheers, Burner.</p><p><br></p></ResortReport>
	<HideReportEDMWeb>False</HideReportEDMWeb>
	<LiftsOpen></LiftsOpen>
	<LiftsStandby></LiftsStandby>
	<LiftsClosed></LiftsClosed>
	<RunsOpen></RunsOpen>
	<RunsGroomed></RunsGroomed>
	<RunsSnowmaking>5</RunsSnowmaking>
	<TerrainParks></TerrainParks>
	<Backcountry>It's a bad day for the backcountry.  The visibility is poor, the weather is wet and the cover is thin and punchy.  Leave it for another day but take care if you must head out there. </Backcountry>
	<Avalanche></Avalanche>
	<HeliLink>Closed</HeliLink>
</SnowReport>
